meta.title: Sophisticated Relative & Noun Clauses
meta.description: Master advanced relative clause structures and complex noun clauses.
meta.description_ko: 고급 관계절 구조와 복잡한 명사절을 학습합니다.

explanation.en: 'To which' = preposition + relative pronoun, used in formal written English.
explanation.ko: 'to which' = 전치사 + 관계 대명사, 격식체 문어에서 사용.

questions.prompt: 'The extent to which social media shapes public opinion is debatable.' — 'to which'의 역할은?

explanation.en: 'The manner in which' = formal relative clause. 'In which' replaces 'in what way'.
explanation.ko: 'the manner in which' = 격식체 관계절. 'in which'는 'in what way'를 대체.

questions.prompt: The manner _____ the evidence was presented undermined the credibility of the prosecution.

explanation.en: 'Whoever' = free relative pronoun meaning 'any person who'.
explanation.ko: 'whoever' = '누구든지'를 의미하는 자유 관계 대명사.

questions.prompt: 'Whoever submits the best proposal will receive the contract.' — 'whoever'의 기능은?
choices: 자유 관계 대명사 (= any person who)

explanation.en: 'Whose' = possessive relative pronoun referring to the scientist's research.
explanation.ko: 'whose' = 과학자의 연구를 지칭하는 소유 관계 대명사.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: The scientist whose research led to the breakthrough was awarded the Nobel Prize.

explanation.en: 'Which' can refer to the entire preceding clause, not just a noun.
explanation.ko: 'which'는 단순한 명사가 아닌 앞 절 전체를 받을 수 있습니다.

questions.prompt: 'He resigned, which came as a surprise to everyone.' — 'which'가 받는 것은?

explanation.en: 'The point that I am trying to make' = restrictive relative clause with 'that'.
explanation.ko: 'the point that I am trying to make' = 'that'을 사용한 한정 관계절.

questions.prompt: The point _____ I am trying to make is that inequality is systemic.

explanation.en: 'Where' replaces 'in which' for places informally; preposition stranding ('which ... in') is also informal.
explanation.ko: 장소에는 'where'가 비격식체에서 'in which'를 대체. 전치사 말미 배치도 비격식체.

questions.prompt: 'The city in which I grew up has changed beyond recognition.' — 이 문장의 더 비격식체 대안은?
choices: The city where I grew up has changed beyond recognition.
The city which I grew up in has changed beyond recognition.
Both A and B are equally informal alternatives.
Neither A nor B is correct.

explanation.en: After 'everything', use 'that' (not 'which') in restrictive relative clauses.
explanation.ko: 'everything' 뒤에는 한정 관계절에서 'which' 대신 'that'을 씁니다.

questions.prompt: Everything _____ she said was recorded and transcribed verbatim.

explanation.en: 'What the report fails to address' = nominal clause functioning as the subject of the sentence.
explanation.ko: 'what the report fails to address' = 문장의 주어로 기능하는 명사절.

questions.prompt: 'What the report fails to address is the long-term environmental impact.' — 'What'절의 문법적 역할은?

explanation.en: 'What remains to be seen' = nominal free relative clause as subject.
explanation.ko: 'what remains to be seen' = 주어로서의 명사적 자유 관계절.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: What remains to be seen is whether the policy will have its intended effect.

explanation.en: 'The time when such issues were ignored' = temporal relative clause using 'when'.
explanation.ko: 'the time when such issues were ignored' = 'when'을 사용한 시간 관계절.

questions.prompt: The time _____ such issues were ignored has long since passed.

explanation.en: 'Whether the data was manipulated' = noun clause acting as subject complement after 'unclear'.
explanation.ko: 'whether the data was manipulated' = 'unclear' 뒤에서 주어 보어로 기능하는 명사절.

questions.prompt: 'It remains unclear whether the data was manipulated.' — 'whether'절은 어떤 절인가요?

explanation.en: Appositive 'that' clause: 'evidence that + clause' = noun + noun clause explaining the noun.
explanation.ko: 동격 'that' 절: 'evidence that + 절' = 명사 + 명사를 설명하는 명사절.

questions.prompt: There is compelling evidence _____ the defendant was present at the scene.

explanation.en: 'Whichever ... you choose' = concessive free relative: regardless of which option.
explanation.ko: 'whichever ... you choose' = 어느 것을 선택하든; 양보적 자유 관계절.

questions.prompt: 'Whichever option you choose, the outcome is likely to be the same.' — 이 문장의 특징은?

explanation.en: 'The fact that + clause' = appositive noun clause explaining 'the fact'.
explanation.ko: 'the fact that + 절' = 'the fact'를 설명하는 동격 명사절.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: The fact that inequality is widening is supported by considerable empirical evidence.

explanation.en: Non-defining relative clause: 'which were considerable' adds extra info about 'the difficulties'.
explanation.ko: 비한정 관계절: 'which were considerable'는 '어려움'에 대한 추가 정보를 제공.

questions.prompt: Notwithstanding the difficulties, _____ were considerable, the team delivered on time.

explanation.en: 'On which' (formal) → 'which ... on' (informal preposition stranding).
explanation.ko: 'on which'(격식) → 'which ... on'(비격식 전치사 말미 배치).

questions.prompt: 'The grounds on which the case was dismissed were procedural.' — 이 구조를 비격식체로 쓰면?
choices: The grounds that the case was dismissed on were procedural.
The grounds the case was dismissed were procedural.
The grounds which was dismissed were procedural.
The grounds where the case dismissed were procedural.

explanation.en: 'Who' refers to people. Companies are things: use 'which' or 'that'.
explanation.ko: 'who'는 사람에게 씁니다. 회사는 사물: 'which' 또는 'that'을 사용.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'The company who manufactures the product has been in business for over a century.'

explanation.en: 'The hypothesis on which / under which' = prepositional relative clause.
explanation.ko: 'the hypothesis on/under which' = 전치사 관계절.

questions.prompt: The hypothesis _____ the researchers are working rests on several unproven assumptions.

explanation.en: 'However + adjective/adverb + subject + verb' = concessive clause (regardless of degree).
explanation.ko: 'however + 형용사/부사 + 주어 + 동사' = 양보절 (정도에 관계없이).

questions.prompt: 'However hard she tried, she could not solve the problem.' — 'However' + 형용사의 기능은?

explanation.en: 'Whoever is responsible' = nominal free relative clause as subject.
explanation.ko: 'whoever is responsible' = 주어로서의 명사적 자유 관계절.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Whoever is responsible for the data breach will be held fully accountable.

explanation.en: 'What the implications might be' = indirect question / nominal clause after 'considering'.
explanation.ko: 'what the implications might be' = 'considering' 뒤의 간접 의문문/명사절.

questions.prompt: It is worth considering _____ the long-term implications of the decision might be.

explanation.en: Non-defining relative clauses add extra, non-essential information and use commas.
explanation.ko: 비한정 관계절은 쉼표를 사용하여 추가적인 비필수 정보를 제공합니다.

questions.prompt: 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? 'A: How should I refer back to the concept I mentioned earlier? B: You could use a _____ relative clause with "which" to add information without defining.'

explanation.en: Advanced relative clause classifications.
explanation.ko: 고급 관계절 분류.

questions.prompt: 관계절 유형과 특징을 연결하세요.
pairs: 명사를 특정하는 필수 정보, 쉼표 없음
추가 정보, 쉼표로 구분, that 불가
